leadText: Blizzard redirects here. For the weather condition, see Hail (weather condition). Blizzard (Japanese: ふぶき Blizzard) is a damage-dealing Ice-type move introduced in Generation I. It is: TM14 from Generation I to Pokémon Ultra Sun and Ultra Moon and in Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl. TM51 in Pokémon: Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee!. TR06 in Pokémon Sword and Shield. TM143 in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet. TM105 in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation I 1.2 Generations II and III 1.3 Generations IV and V 1.4 Generation VI onward 1.5 Generation VIII 1.5.1 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.6 Generation IX 1.6.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Generation I [ edit | edit source ] Blizzard does damage and has a 10% chance of freezing the target. It has an accuracy of 90% in Generation I. In the Japanese versions of the Generation I core series games, Blizzard had a 30% chance of freezing. The chance was lowered to 10% for Pokémon Yellow 's Colosseum 2 , the Stadium games , and all international Generation I games. Generations II and III [ edit | edit source ] The accuracy of Blizzard was reduced to 70%. Blizzard will hit both opponents in a Double Battle . Blizzard can be used as the second move of a Pokémon Contest combination , with the user gaining 4 bonus appeal points if Hail or Powder Snow was used in the previous turn. Generations IV and V [ edit | edit source ] When used during hail , Blizzard bypasses accuracy checks to always hit, unless the target is in the semi-invulnerable turn of a move such as Dig or Fly . In Pokémon Diamond and Pearl , due to a glitch, when used in hail, it has a 30% chance to hit a target that has used Protect or Detect ; this was fixed in Pokémon Platinum onward. In Triple Battles , Blizzard will only hit opposing adjacent Pokémon. Generation VI onward [ edit | edit source ] Blizzard's power was lowered from 120 to 110. In Horde Encounters , it will target all opponents. Blizzard will also knock down Berries growing on Berry trees found in the background of battles . Blizzard can also be used as part of a Contest Spectacular combination , with the user gaining extra three appeal points if the move Hail was used in the prior turn. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Arceus [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Arceus Category Special PP 5 Agile Strong Power 100 80 120 Accuracy 75% 75% 90% Action speed modifier Self 0 0 +5 Target 0 +3 0 Additional effect Chance 30% 30% 50% Turns 3 2 4 ? ? Inflicts the target with frostbite Like all other moves in Pokémon Legends: Arceus, Blizzard only hits one target. Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] When used during snow , Blizzard bypasses accuracy checks to always hit, unless the target is in the semi-invulnerable turn of a move such as Dig or Fly . Blizzard is considered a wind move , and will activate Wind Power or Wind Rider if a Pokémon with that Ability is hit with it. Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Blizzard functions similarly to Thunder and Hurricane (coincidentally all moves that have 70% accuracy in the turn-based games). Upon the wind-up completing a small, snowy wind effect appears underneath the target's current location, which intensifies into the actual blizzard to deal damage after a delay. Pokémon Legends: Z-A Category Special Accuracy 100% Standard Plus Move Power 110 132 Cooldown 12s Move wind-up time 35 frames Effect duration —s Additional effect Chance 10% — — May freeze the target.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description Stad The strongest Ice -type attack. Has a one-in-ten chance of freezing the target solid. Stad2 The strongest Ice -type attack. Has a one-in-ten chance of freezing the target. G S C An attack that may freeze the foe. R S E Colo XD Hits the foe with an icy storm that may freeze it. (move) A vicious snow-and-wind attack that may freeze the foe. R S Colo XD (TM) A brutal snow-and-wind attack that may freeze the foe. E (TM) FR LG The foe is blasted with a blizzard. It may freeze the foe solid. D P Pt HG SS PBR A howling blizzard is summoned to strike the foe. It may also freeze the target solid. B W B2 W2 A howling blizzard is summoned to strike the opposing team. It may also freeze them solid. X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP S V A howling blizzard is summoned to strike opposing Pokémon. This may also leave the opposing Pokémon frozen. LA A howling blizzard is summoned to strike the target. This may also leave the target with frostbite. ZA The user attacks by blasting targets with a howling blizzard. This may also leave targets frozen.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B Inflicts damage on the target. It may also leave the target frozen and incapable of action. MD T D Inflicts damage on all enemies in the room. It may also afflict enemies with a Frozen status condition. A Pokémon with the Frozen status is incapable of action. It never misses during a Hail condition. MD S Inflicts damage on all enemies in the room. It may also afflict enemies with a Frozen status condition. A Pokémon with the Frozen status is incapable of action. This move never misses during a Hail condition. B S L おなじへやの てきポケモンに ダメージをあたえ いっていのかくりつで こおりじょうたいにかえる こおりじょうたいの ポケモンは こうどうできない てんきが『あられ』だと かならず めいちゅうする Conq. A howling blizzard is summoned to strike the opposing team. It may also freeze them solid. MD GtI It damages enemies in the same room. It could also cause the Frozen status condition. With the Frozen status, they can't do anything. S MD MD RT DX It damages enemies in the same room. It could also make them frozen. During hail, the move never misses!

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] The English manual for Pokémon Yellow mentions that " Blizzard 's probability of freezing changes by 10%" when used in Colosseum 2 . This is actually a mistranslation of something that only occurs in the Japanese version. In the Japanese Generation I handheld games, Blizzard has a 30% chance of freezing its target, but in Pokémon Yellow's Colosseum 2 this is reduced to 10%; in international releases, Blizzard always has a 10% chance of freezing its target. Save for Snom , every Ice-type is capable of learning Blizzard.
